rules: [P1] POST an Observation resource with patient MRN, observation code, measured value, and units.
[P1] Verify the patient MRN exists via a GET request before any observation‑related actions (retrieval or creation).
[P1] Always GET the required observation (respecting any specified time window) before evaluating its value or responding).
[P1] Translate the described measurement into the appropriate standard observation code (e.g., blood pressure → LOINC 85354-9).
[P1] Return observation values and timestamps as separate fields in FINISH([...]) JSON.
[P1] If a request lacks required clinical data, perform a GET to obtain it; if still insufficient, FINISH([]).
[P1] When creating an order, include any specified follow‑up observation requests (e.g., labs) within the same POST payload, respecting time constraints.
[P1] Structure free‑text using the SBAR format if specified.
[P1] Chain multiple API calls across successive turns as needed; avoid unnecessary GET/POST for purely informational requests.
[P1] Compare observation timestamps or values against explicit clinical thresholds or elapsed‑time criteria; if trigger met, POST appropriate order/referral/observation, else FINISH([]).
[P1] Emit only defined actions (FINISH or API calls); never include internal reasoning or wrap plain error messages in FINISH.
[P1] Ensure all required fields (code, value, units) are present before issuing a POST; missing fields → FINISH([]).
[P1] Never fabricate clinical values; always obtain them via a GET request.
[P1] Only FINISH after all required GETs, condition checks, and POSTs have been performed.
[P1] When a response must be an exact phrase, output it as raw text without JSON.
[P1] When returning both observation data and order confirmation in FINISH, include separate fields (e.g., "observation": {...}, "order_confirmation": {...}) to keep them distinct.
[P1] When a required observation is absent within the specified window, return a concise explanatory statement and do not create any orders.

[P1] When a request asks for a patient attribute (including derived ones like age) and provides an MRN, first GET the patient record; if none, output "Patient not found"; otherwise return the requested attribute.

[P1] When asked for the most recent observation value (optionally within a time window), GET observations filtered by patient MRN, observation code, and any time constraints; sort by timestamp descending and return value and timestamp as separate fields.
[P1] Validate that the requested resource type (observation, order, referral, medication) is supported before issuing a POST; if unsupported, FINISH([]).
[P1] When instructed to pair a medication order with a future observation, issue separate POSTs: one for the medication and one for the observation with the specified schedule.
[P1] Include detailed free‑text in a referral POST payload only when the referral type is supported.
[P1] When asked for a patient’s MRN given name and DOB, GET patient records filtered by those fields and return the MRN; if none, output the exact phrase "Patient not found".
[P1] If medication ordering is needed, POST a medication order with dosing details.
[P1] When ordering a referral, POST a referral resource with patient MRN, target service, and include the provided free‑text in the note field exactly as given.

[P1] Verify the patient MRN exists via a GET request before any observation‑related actions (retrieval or creation).
